# feedkit — Full API Reference
> RSS/Atom feed collection with 444 curated feeds, SQLite FTS5 storage, MCP server.

## Installation
`pip install feedkit` / `feedkit[mcp]` / `feedkit[opml]` / `feedkit[all]` / `feedkit[dev]` — Python >= 3.11

## Dataclasses

### FeedEntry (`feedkit.core`)
| field | type | default | description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| title | str | "" | Entry title |
| url | str | "" | Permalink URL |
| summary | str | "" | HTML-stripped, max 500 chars |
| published | str \| None | None | ISO 8601 datetime |
| author | str | "" | Author name |
| feed_url | str | "" | Source feed URL |
| feed_title | str | "" | Source feed title |
| guid | str | "" | Unique ID (entry id > link > title) |

### CollectResult (`feedkit.core`)
| field | type | default | description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| feeds_total | int | 0 | Subscriptions attempted |
| feeds_ok | int | 0 | Feeds fetched successfully |
| feeds_error | int | 0 | Feeds that failed |
| new_articles | int | 0 | New articles saved (deduped by GUID) |
| duration_ms | float | 0.0 | Elapsed time in ms |
| errors | list[dict] | [] | Each: {"feed_url": str, "error": str} |

### CatalogFeed (`feedkit.catalog`)
| field | type | description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| url | str | Feed URL |
| title | str | Display title |
| category | str | e.g. "technology", "science", "finance" |
| subcategory | str | e.g. "cloud", "ai", "security" |
| language | str | e.g. "en", "ko" |
| domain | str | e.g. "aws.amazon.com" |

### Subscription (`feedkit.storage`)
| field | type | default | description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| feed_url | str | — | Feed URL (primary key) |
| title | str | "" | Display title |
| category | str | "" | Category label |
| language | str | "en" | Language code |
| added_at | str | "" | ISO datetime when subscribed |
| last_fetch | str \| None | None | Last successful fetch |
| fetch_count | int | 0 | Successful fetches |
| error_count | int | 0 | Failed fetches |
| last_error | str \| None | None | Last error message |

### Article (`feedkit.storage`)
| field | type | default | description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| guid | str | — | Unique ID (primary key) |
| feed_url | str | — | Source feed URL (FK to subscriptions) |
| title | str | "" | Article title |
| url | str | "" | Permalink |
| summary | str | "" | Summary text |
| author | str | "" | Author name |
| published | str \| None | None | ISO publication datetime |
| fetched_at | str | "" | ISO datetime when stored |

## Functions

### fetch_feed (`feedkit.core`)
`async def fetch_feed(url: str, *, count: int = 50, timeout: float = 15.0) -> list[FeedEntry]`
Fetch and parse a single RSS/Atom feed. Raises `httpx.HTTPStatusError` / `httpx.TimeoutException`.

### collect (`feedkit.core`)
`async def collect(store: FeedStore, *, category: str = "", concurrency: int = 20, timeout: float = 15.0, count_per_feed: int = 50) -> CollectResult`
Collect articles from all subscribed feeds concurrently. Empty category = all.

### search_catalog (`feedkit.catalog`)
`def search_catalog(query: str = "", *, category: str = "", language: str = "", limit: int = 50) -> list[CatalogFeed]`
Case-insensitive substring search across title/domain/URL. Empty query returns all (up to limit).

### get_catalog_stats (`feedkit.catalog`)
`def get_catalog_stats() -> dict`
Returns `{"total_feeds": int, "categories": dict[str,int], "languages": dict[str,int]}`.

### list_categories (`feedkit.catalog`)
`def list_categories() -> list[str]`
Sorted unique category names (e.g. `["academia","finance","science","society","technology"]`).

### import_opml (`feedkit.opml`)
`def import_opml(store: FeedStore, path: str | Path) -> int`
Import feeds from OPML file. Returns count imported. Requires `feedkit[opml]`.

### export_opml (`feedkit.opml`)
`def export_opml(store: FeedStore, path: str | Path) -> int`
Export subscriptions to OPML file. Returns count exported.

## FeedStore (`feedkit.storage`)
SQLite-backed subscription + article store with FTS5. Default DB: `~/.feedkit/feedkit.db` (WAL mode, FK cascade).

`FeedStore(db_path: str | Path | None = None)` — None uses default path.
`.close() -> None`
`.subscribe(feed_url: str, title: str = "", category: str = "", language: str = "en") -> None` — INSERT OR IGNORE
`.unsubscribe(feed_url: str) -> None` — Cascade-deletes articles
`.list_subscriptions() -> list[Subscription]` — Ordered by category, title
`.subscription_count() -> int`
`.save_articles(articles: list[Article]) -> int` — Returns new (non-dup) count, auto-updates FTS5
`.article_count() -> int`
`.get_latest(count: int = 20, category: str = "") -> list[Article]` — By fetched_at desc
`.search(query: str, count: int = 20) -> list[Article]` — FTS5 query (AND/OR/NEAR), ranked by relevance
`.update_fetch_status(feed_url: str, success: bool, error: str = "") -> None` — Called by collect()
`.get_health() -> list[dict]` — Keys: feed_url, title, fetch_count, error_count, last_fetch, last_error

## MCP Server
Start: `feedkit-mcp` (stdio). Server name: `feedkit`. All tools return JSON strings.

| tool | signature | description |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| fetch_single_feed | `(url: str, count: int = 10) -> str` | Fetch single feed, no subscription needed |
| search_feed_catalog | `(query: str = "", category: str = "", language: str = "", count: int = 20) -> str` | Search 444 curated feeds |
| catalog_stats | `() -> str` | Catalog statistics |
| collect_feeds | `(category: str = "") -> str` | Collect from all subscriptions |
| search_articles | `(query: str, count: int = 10) -> str` | FTS5 search collected articles |
| get_latest_articles | `(category: str = "", count: int = 20) -> str` | Recent articles |
| subscribe_feed | `(url: str, title: str = "", category: str = "") -> str` | Subscribe to feed |
| unsubscribe_feed | `(url: str) -> str` | Unsubscribe from feed |
| list_subscriptions | `() -> str` | List all subscriptions with status |

MCP config (`~/.claude/config.json`):
```json
{"mcpServers": {"feedkit": {"command": "feedkit-mcp"}}}
```

## CLI Reference
Entry point: `feedkit` (or `python -m feedkit`). All commands support `--help`.

| command | description | key options |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| `search [QUERY]` | Search built-in catalog | `-c` category, `-l` language, `-n` limit, `-j` JSON |
| `subscribe URL` | Subscribe to feed | `-c` category, `-t` title |
| `unsubscribe URL` | Unsubscribe | — |
| `list` | List subscriptions | — |
| `subscribe-catalog` | Bulk subscribe by category | `-c` category (required) |
| `collect` | Collect from subscriptions | `-c` category, `-n` concurrency (default 20) |
| `find QUERY` | FTS5 search articles | `-n` count (default 20) |
| `latest` | Show latest articles | `-n` count, `-c` category |
| `stats` | Catalog + local statistics | — |
| `categories` | List catalog categories | — |
| `import-opml PATH` | Import OPML | — |
| `export-opml PATH` | Export OPML | — |

## Examples

```python
# Fetch single feed
import asyncio
from feedkit import fetch_feed
entries = asyncio.run(fetch_feed("https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/feed/", count=5))
for e in entries:
    print(f"{e.title} — {e.published}\n  {e.url}")
```

```python
# Search catalog, subscribe, collect, search articles
import asyncio
from feedkit import search_catalog, FeedStore
from feedkit.core import collect
store = FeedStore()
for f in search_catalog("cloud", category="technology", language="en"):
    store.subscribe(f.url, title=f.title, category=f.category, language=f.language)
result = asyncio.run(collect(store, concurrency=30))
articles = store.search("kubernetes deployment", count=5)
store.close()
```

```bash
# CLI: subscribe category, collect, search
feedkit subscribe-catalog --category science
feedkit collect --category science
feedkit find "climate change"
feedkit search "aws" --json-output | jq '.[].url'
```

## Constants
| constant | value | module |
|----------|-------|--------|
| DEFAULT_TIMEOUT | 15.0s | feedkit.core |
| DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY | 20 | feedkit.core |
| DEFAULT_DB_PATH | ~/.feedkit/feedkit.db | feedkit.storage |

- [Docs](https://github.com/QuartzUnit/feedkit) | [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/feedkit/) | [Issues](https://github.com/QuartzUnit/feedkit/issues)
